summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that the LocalExecutor provides zero sandboxing, allowing arbitrary Python code execution including reading protected files, executing system commands, and accessing environment variables.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that LocalExecutor.exec() can read /etc/passwd, execute system commands via os.popen('whoami'), and access environment variables, proving the executor provides no isolation from the host system.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned the target repository, built the reproduction image, and executed the exploit script. The LocalExecutor pattern successfully read /etc/passwd, executed system commands, and confirmed full builtins exposure, demonstrating zero sandboxing.
verification_method: The exploit script reads the actual source code from /target-repo/openchatbi/code/local_executor.py, asserts it contains the vulnerable exec() pattern with full builtins, then replicates the exact same code pattern to demonstrate arbitrary code execution. The proof strings (EVOHUNT_PROOF) distinguish successful exploitation from unrelated errors or startup failures.
evidence_produced_by_run: The exploit verified that local_executor.py contains exec(code, safe_globals, safe_globals) with __builtins__: __builtins__.
The exploit verified that run_python_code.py contains multiple LocalExecutor() fallback paths.
Arbitrary code execution was confirmed by reading /etc/passwd and executing system commands through the exec() path.
Full builtins exposure was confirmed: __import__, open, eval are all accessible through the safe_globals namespace.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository from GitHub, checks out the main branch, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
